conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

Let me first check the official eligibility rules so I can properly assess your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I've got three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So you have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to gather some information about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. So Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2 - all under 16.

Next question: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Now I need to ask: **Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Are any of the children currently in hospital for more than 12 weeks, or in care for more than 8 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in hospital or care. They're all healthy and at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's the assessment for each child:

**Alex (age 3):** ✅ Eligible - Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for their care.

**Blake (age 8):** ✅ Eligible - Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for their care.

**Charlie (age 2):** ✅ Eligible - Charlie is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for their care.

Since all three children live with you, are under 16, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them, you meet all the eligibility criteria.

**Please note:** Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child. If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application for all three children.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

All children are under 16 years old, live with the claimant, are not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for all three children.
